prompt: A vast cinematic landscape where thousands of people form a glowing river-like flow across a desert terrain, viewed from high above. The entire mass clearly moves in one unified direction, like a luminous current cutting through the land. Within this flow, natural swirling counter-currents emerge — clusters of people visibly moving in the opposite direction, forming embedded vortices. These opposing motions are part of the same system, not separated. The paradox must feel inevitable: the global direction is undeniable, yet local motion contradicts it simultaneously. Subtle surrealism: light trails bend inconsistently and the terrain gently warps to support conflicting flows. Style: hyper-realistic, cinematic, volumetric lighting, atmospheric haze, high detail, slightly dreamlike but grounded. --no text, numbers, charts, symbols, diagrams
visual_logic: A unified human flow moves one way while embedded counter-currents move oppositely, making local truths contradict the global pattern simultaneously.
